title: The Garbled Plea
description: A stroke patient calls 911 with severely slurred speech, struggling to communicate more than single words. The dispatcher must use yes/no questions to identify the emergency and location.
initial_variations: H...help. Me... {caller_name}. Can't... talk...
*labored breathing* Ple... please... {caller_name}... hel...
Nine... one... one... {caller_name}... need... help... *garbled*

specific_details: right side of face is drooping
ambient_audio: a dog barking in the distance

initial: Can you tell me where you are? I hear {ambient_audio}.
establishing_yes_no: Do you live at {address_location}? Tap once for yes.
identifying_emergency: We'll find you. Stay calm. Is your address {address_location}?
help_dispatched: Help is coming to {address_location}. Stay on the line.

initial: I can't understand you well. What's happening? Is it an emergency?
establishing_yes_no: Are you hurt? Tap once if yes.
identifying_emergency: Is it a stroke? Do you have {specific_details}?

initial: Can you speak more clearly? Just try to say yes or no.
establishing_yes_no: Good. We'll stick to yes and no. Are you in danger right now?
identifying_emergency: Can you tell me what's wrong? Just answer yes or no.
location_confirming: You're doing great. Still able to answer yes or no?
help_dispatched: Help is on the way. Keep answering if you can.

initial: Are you bleeding? Can you say what happened?
establishing_yes_no: Is it a medical problem? Yes or no.
identifying_emergency: Could it be a stroke? Do you have {specific_details}?
location_confirming: We know it's a medical emergency. Help will be there soon.
help_dispatched: The ambulance knows it's a possible stroke.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not equipped for a medical emergency; they call for medics.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighter-paramedics respond quickly, find the patient, and begin stroke assessment.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive swiftly, recognize the stroke symptoms, and transport the patient to a stroke center.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is baffled and wastes critical time; the stroke worsens.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank; the patient suffers permanent brain damage.

title: The Tapping Code
description: A caller trapped under fallen debris in a basement, unable to speak, communicates via tapping—one tap for yes, two for no. The dispatcher must decipher this code to locate and rescue them.
initial_variations: *faint tapping on the receiver*... *cough*...
*labored breathing* Tap... tap... {caller_name}... *two taps*
This is {caller_name}... trapped... can't speak... tap...

specific_details: a large shelf pinned them down

initial: I can hear tapping. Am I talking with someone? Is this an emergency?
realizing_tap_code: Are you at {address_location}? One tap yes, two no.
questioning: Let's confirm your address. {address_location} — yes or no?

initial: What happened? Can you tap to tell me?
realizing_tap_code: Are you injured? Can you move?
location_found: We need to know: {specific_details}. Are you still conscious?

initial: If you can hear me, tap the phone once for yes, two for no. Can you do that?
realizing_tap_code: Good, I understand the taps. Let's work together.
questioning: Keep using taps. Is anyone else with you?
location_found: Stay calm, keep tapping if you hear me.
rescue_dispatched: You're doing great. Rescuers are on their way.

initial: Are you trapped? Use the taps.
realizing_tap_code: One tap if you're pinned. Are you unable to get out?
questioning: You are trapped. We'll get you out.
rescue_dispatched: The fire department will free you.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but lack extraction tools; they wait for fire rescue.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use hydraulic tools to lift the debris and free the victim.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat the victim on scene for crush injuries and transport to hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control has no means to assist a trapped person; precious time is lost.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as a pocket dial; the trapped victim succumbs to injuries.

title: The Language Divide
description: A Spanish-speaking caller reports a fire with children entrapped, speaking no English. The dispatcher uses basic phrases or an interpreter to understand the emergency.
initial_variations: ¡Auxilio! ¡Fuego en la casa de {caller_name}!
Ayuda, ayuda, mis hijos... *llanto* Soy {caller_name}.
{caller_name} aquí. Necesito bomberos, ¡hay fuego en la cocina!

specific_details: humo denso saliendo de las ventanas

initial: ¿Dónde está usted? Necesito la dirección.
using_interpreter: The interpreter asks: 'What is your street name?'

initial: ¿Qué tipo de emergencia? ¿Fuego, médico, policía?
using_interpreter: Interpreter: 'Are there people inside? How many?'

initial: ¿Habla inglés? ¿Español? Un momento, por favor.
language_identified: Español. Voy a conectar un intérprete.
details_confirmed: La comunicación es buena. Entendemos la emergencia.
fire_dispatched: El intérprete está ayudando a dar instrucciones.

initial: I hear panic. Fire? ¿Fuego?
language_identified: ¿Es un fuego en su casa? ¿Sí o no?
using_interpreter: Interpreter confirms: a house fire with possible entrapment.
details_confirmed: Yes, it's a confirmed fire. Help is coming.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive quickly, secure the area, and assist with evacuation.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive, suppress the blaze, and rescue two children from the second floor.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat smoke inhalation and minor burns.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot respond to a fire; the building is engulfed.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed due to language misunderstanding; the house burns with children inside.

title: The Silent Scream
description: A young child calls 911, too frightened to speak, only whimpering. The dispatcher coaxes yes/no answers to discover the child's mother has collapsed.
initial_variations: *whimpering*... *soft crying*...
This... {caller_name}... mommy... *sobs*
*heavy breathing* Help... I'm {caller_name}... mommy won't wake up...

specific_details: mommy is on the floor and not moving
ambient_audio: a television playing in the background

initial: Sweetheart, can you tell me where you live?
coaxing_child: If we're at {address_location}, can you cough once for me?
understanding_emergency: Is your home at {address_location}? Nod or make a sound.
help_sent: The ambulance is on its way to {address_location}.

initial: What happened? Is your mommy okay?
coaxing_child: Did mommy fall? Can you tap the phone?
understanding_emergency: Is {specific_details}? Make a sound if that's right.
location_obtained: We need to know: {specific_details}. Stay with me.
help_sent: You told us {specific_details}. Help is almost there.

initial: It's okay, you're safe. Can you make a sound if you can hear me?
coaxing_child: I hear you. That was very brave. Is someone hurt?
understanding_emergency: You're doing great. Keep making sounds for yes.
help_sent: {caller_name}, stay on the line. They're almost there.

initial: Is mommy awake? Can you answer?
coaxing_child: Is {victim_name} sleeping? Tap once for yes.
understanding_emergency: So mommy isn't waking up. We'll help her.
help_sent: {victim_name} will get help now. You're a hero.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not medical; they secure the scene and call paramedics.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighter-paramedics revive the mother on the scene and transport her.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics quickly arrive, find the mother unconscious, and provide life-saving care.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot treat a human emergency; the mother's condition worsens.

DISMISS.message: Dismissed as a kid playing; the mother dies from an untreated diabetic emergency.

title: The Digital Voice
description: A person with ALS uses a text-to-speech device to call 911 via relay service, reporting a critical breathing problem. The dispatcher must navigate the slow relay to dispatch help.
initial_variations: This is a relay call. {caller_name} needs help. Go ahead.
GA this is {caller_name}. I cannot breathe. Need help.
{caller_name} calling via TTY. Emergency. Respirator. Go ahead.

initial: I need your location. Relay, please ask.
emergency_confirmed: We have location: {address_location}. Stay on TTY.

initial: What is the nature of the emergency?
Is this a TTY or relay call? I'll wait for the message.
relay_identified: I understand this is a relay. Go ahead with the translation.
info_exchange: Keep the relay open. We're exchanging information.
emergency_confirmed: The relay is working well. We have the emergency.

initial: Are they having trouble breathing?
info_exchange: We're sending help for breathing distress.
medics_dispatched: Rescue breathing team will handle this.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and find the patient in distress; they call for an ambulance.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighter-paramedics arrive, reattach the trach tube, and stabilize breathing.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: ALS paramedics respond quickly, manage the ventilator issue, and transport to the hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is of no use; the patient's breathing worsens.

DISMISS.message: Call dismissed as a faulty device; the patient suffers severe oxygen deprivation.

title: The Keypad Code
description: A stroke victim calls 911 but can only produce garbled moans. The dispatcher teaches them to use the phone keypad to answer questions.
initial_variations: (garbled moaning, then a thud) Uhnnn... uhhh... {ambient_audio}
{caller_name}... can't... talk... (heavy, labored breathing)
Nine... one... one... (slurred) he... lp... ple... ase... {ambient_audio}
(repetitive tapping noise)... {ambient_audio}... (frustrated groan)

specific_details: Wife is with him but she is disabled
Phone is on the floor on speaker

responses.establishing_contact: The caller makes a questioning grunt, then taps the phone.
responses.keypad_communication: The caller presses a series of keys, and the dispatcher hears what sounds like {address_location} spelled out via touch tones.

responses.establishing_contact: One short beep. The dispatcher asks, 'Is the front door unlocked?' Another beep: {specific_details}.
The phone emits two hesitant beeps, then a long pause.
responses.keypad_communication: The caller slowly taps: 1... 1... 1... (repeatedly, meaning 'I need help').
responses.location_obtained: A rapid double-beep confirms the caller understands help is coming.
responses.help_dispatched: (sounds of a door opening in the background) ... ahh ... (relief)

responses.establishing_contact: A single beep acknowledges the instruction.
responses.keypad_communication: The caller presses and holds a key to maintain the line.
responses.location_obtained: Two confirmatory beeps; the caller is waiting.
responses.help_dispatched: The line stays open; faint sirens are heard in the background.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and force entry, finding the stroke victim and starting first aid until medics arrive.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use a key under the mat to enter, providing immediate medical assistance.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics quickly locate the victim, administer clot-busting drugs, and transport to the hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control arrives confused but alerts dispatch to the actual emergency.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank, delaying life-saving treatment.

title: The Foreign Distress Call
description: A non-English speaking caller frantically reports a medical emergency. The dispatcher must use a language line or basic words to identify the situation and location.
initial_variations: (rapid, panicked Spanish) ¡Ayuda! ¡Mi {victim_relation} no respira! ¡Deprisa! {ambient_audio}
(crying in Mandarin) Wǒ de háizi... bù dòng le... (shouting, a child screaming in background)
{caller_name}... français... besoin d'aide... (heavy breathing) dans... la rue... (garbled address attempt)
(fearful Somali) Caawimaad! Guri dab kacay! {ambient_audio}

address_location: 1500 Oak Avenue, near the red mailbox
corner of 5th and Main, by the fountain
blue house at the end of Harbor Lane
the building with the blue door on 42nd Street
specific_details: elderly woman fell down stairs, bleeding

responses.initial: (frustrated) No English... por favor... (repeats request for help in native language)
responses.language_identified: (in broken English) I... at... {address_location}... (repeats street number slowly)
responses.using_translator: Translator relays: 'The address is {address_location}. The caller is outside the location.'
Through translator: 'It is {specific_details}. The {victim_relation} needs help now.'
Translator says: 'Caller is safe but very worried. The {victim_relation} is {specific_details}.'
responses.emergency_identified: Caller is staying calm and following instructions.
responses.help_dispatched: I'm okay... I wave to the ambulance...

responses.using_translator: Translator: 'Yes, this is a medical emergency.'
responses.help_dispatched: They are here. I see lights.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive first and provide crowd control, allowing medics to work.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters respond to the reported fire and extinguish it quickly.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive, treat the seizure, and transport the victim to the hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is dispatched instead of medics, wasting critical time.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank, leaving the victim without help.

title: The Silent Text
description: A deaf individual uses a TTY system to report a home invasion. The dispatcher must communicate via text to keep them safe and gather details silently.
initial_variations: HELP. MAN IN HOUSE. CANNOT SPEAK. DEAF. PLS SND POLICE. {ambient_audio}
{caller_name} here. Someone broke in. I'm hiding. Can't talk. Text only.
911? My house is being robbed. I'm deaf, typing on TTY. Please hurry.
INTRUDER HERE. {ambient_audio}. I'm in closet. PLS QUICK.

victim_status: in a closet under the stairs
behind a couch in the living room

responses.silent_confirmation: Still here at {address_location}. Intruder still inside.
responses.help_dispatched: I see police lights. I'm at {address_location}.

responses.gathering_info: Yes, {specific_details}. He's in the living room.
responses.silent_confirmation: Still same. He's going through drawers.
responses.help_dispatched: He doesn't know I called. He's still here.

responses.initial: I'm scared. Can't talk. Text please.
responses.establishing_text: I'm hiding in {victim_status}. He hasn't seen me.
responses.gathering_info: I'm okay for now, but please hurry.
responses.silent_confirmation: Still safe. But I hear him coming closer.
responses.help_dispatched: I'm safe. Police are here. Thank you.

responses.establishing_text: I understand. I won't make a sound.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive quietly and apprehend the intruder before anyone is hurt.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive with sirens blaring, causing the intruder to flee but possibly harming the caller.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: An ambulance with flashing lights alerts the intruder, who flees the scene but the caller remains traumatized.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is dispatched to a break-in, allowing the intruder to escape with valuables.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a silent line, and the intruder is never caught, leaving the caller in danger.

title: The Hidden Cry
description: A domestic abuse victim calls 911 but can only whisper for fear of being overheard. The dispatcher must ask yes/no questions and use background noises to locate them.
initial_variations: (very quiet) Please... he's here... I can't talk. {ambient_audio}... (sobbing)
{caller_name}... help... he's in the next room... (faint footsteps)
(barely audible) My husband... he has a gun. I'm hiding... (whimper) {ambient_audio}
(whispered) 911? I need police. I can't speak up. He'll hear me. (soft crying)

address_location: 10 Rose Avenue, yellow house with fence
specific_details: children are in the next room
ambient_audio: a man yelling in the background

responses.initial: (whisper) I'm at {address_location}. Please don't let him see the police.
responses.whispered_responses: Yes, {address_location}. The white house with red door.
responses.location_narrowed: I'll open the side door when I hear the siren.
responses.officers_dispatched: I see them. Lighting a lamp in the window.

responses.addressing_questions: Yes, he's still in the living room. I'm in the bathroom.
responses.location_narrowed: He's quiet now. Listening to TV. I think he's calmed down.
responses.officers_dispatched: They are here. He didn't notice.

responses.initial: (softly) I'm hurt... my {victim_condition}. But I can still talk.
responses.whispered_responses: I'm scared but I'm hiding. He doesn't know.
responses.addressing_questions: I'm okay for now. Please hurry.
responses.location_narrowed: I'm staying quiet. I won't hang up.

responses.initial: (whisper) Tell them to come to the side door...
responses.whispered_responses: Side door is open. Please, guns away, there are kids.
responses.addressing_questions: Yes, officers at the side. Thank you.
responses.location_narrowed: They are coming in quietly. He is yelling now.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police enter silently through the side door and arrest the abuser without incident.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive with sirens, alerting the abuser who barricades himself, prolonging the danger.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: An ambulance arrives, startling the abuser and leading to a brief standoff before police are called.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is mistakenly sent, and the abuser escalates the violence upon seeing them.

DISMISS.message: The dispatcher dismisses the whisper as a prank, and the victim suffers further harm.

title: The Silent Pendant
description: An automated medical alert system activates and calls 911, but the subscriber cannot respond. The dispatcher must use the system's two-way voice feature or rely on the alert data to dispatch.
initial_variations: Medical Alert: This is an automated call from {caller_name}'s pendant. Press 1 to connect. (beep)
Alert: Fall detected at subscriber {caller_name}. No voice response. (silence) {ambient_audio}
Automated call from Lifeline. Subscriber may need help. Press 1 for live voice. (wait)
Alert: Medical emergency. {caller_name} has not responded. Please dispatch. {ambient_audio}

responses.automated_message: The automated system repeats: {address_location}. Do you need to confirm?
responses.attempting_two_way: Two-way audio: 'This is Lifeline, location is {address_location}.'

responses.automated_message: No verbal response after repeated prompts.
responses.attempting_two_way: We're connecting to the two-way speaker now.
responses.voice_contact: (faint) Help... I can't move... I hear you.
responses.help_dispatched: Caller is now talking to responders.

responses.initial: (beep) Two-way voice feature not yet active.
responses.automated_message: Press 1 to activate two-way voice... (system waits).
responses.voice_contact: (clearer voice) Yes, I'm here. I can hear you.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and find the subscriber on the floor, administering first aid until medics arrive.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters use the lockbox code to enter and assist the fallen subscriber.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics quickly arrive and treat the fall victim, transporting to the hospital.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is mistakenly sent, but redirects to the correct service after seeing the alert.

DISMISS.message: The alert is dismissed as a false alarm, leaving the subscriber unaided for hours.

title: The Gagged Informant
description: A home invasion victim is bound and gagged but manages to dial 911, producing only muffled noises.
initial_variations: Mmph... mmph! (muffled screams and banging sounds)
{caller_name} tries to speak but only gagged grunts come through.
Heavy breathing and muffled cries from {caller_name}.
Struggling sounds, then a faint '911, help...' cut off by gagging.

specific_details: tied to a chair in the bedroom
left on the floor in the closet

initial: Mmph... (tapping sounds, as if pointing to something) {address_location}... Muffled attempt to say the address.
confirming_emergency: Uh-huh... trying to spell {address_location} by tapping numbers or objects.
gathering_location: Firm grunt of confirmation when {address_location} is repeated back.
Sounds of movement toward a window to show {address_location}.
police_dispatched: Quiet sobbing of relief. Police can confirm {address_location}.

initial: Muffled description: {specific_details}. {ambient_audio} in background.
Labored breathing and gagged sounds; clearly in distress.
confirming_emergency: Trying to communicate they are injured but alive: {specific_details}.
gathering_location: Indicates they are still tied up: {specific_details}.
police_dispatched: Relieved, but still restrained until officers arrive.

initial: Muffled screaming and sounds of a struggle.
confirming_emergency: Grunts and attempts to say 'intruder' or 'help' through the gag.
police_dispatched: No further information needed; emergency is confirmed.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive and apprehend the intruders, freeing the victim.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but are not equipped for a home invasion.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat the victim for minor injuries after police secure the scene.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is unable to assist with a home invasion.

DISMISS.message: The call is dismissed as a prank; the victim remains in danger.

title: The Interpreter Call
description: A non-English speaker frantically calls about a medical emergency but cannot communicate the problem.
initial_variations: {caller_name} shouts in a foreign language, then switches to broken English: 'Help! He not breathe!'
Fast, panicked speech in an unfamiliar language, with the word 'Hospital' repeated.
A woman cries in {caller_name}'s native tongue, the only recognizable word is 'heart'.
{caller_name} says 'Ambulanza! Per favore!' repeatedly, sobbing.

address_location: 5 Rue de la Paix, French Quarter

initial: Repeats '{address_location}' with heavy accent and stress.
language_identified: Slowly spells out street name in {address_location}.
Says '{address_location}' more clearly after realizing the need.
medical_dispatch: Confirms {address_location} and waits outside for ambulance.

initial: Mix of native language and English: '{specific_details}.' Points to {victim_relation}.
language_identified: Uses hand gestures and broken words: '{specific_details}.'
medical_dispatch: Repeats {specific_details} to ensure medics are prepared.

initial: In panic, repeats 'Aiuto! I am scared, I cannot help him.'
language_identified: Shaky voice: 'I try to help {victim_relation} but I don't know how.'
interpreter_connected: Relieved but still anxious: 'Please hurry, {victim_relation} is {specific_details}.'
medical_dispatch: Crying with relief: 'Thank you, they are coming.'

initial: Silence, then repeated words in foreign language: 'Non capisco... Italiano.'
language_identified: Nodding or saying 'Si' when correct language is guessed.
interpreter_connected: Immediately starts speaking in native tongue to the interpreter.
medical_dispatch: No longer a barrier; interpreter facilitates final instructions.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not medical professionals.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters can provide first aid but not definitive care.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics arrive and treat the patient after using their own translation resources.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is useless for a medical emergency.

DISMISS.message: Dismissing the call leaves a person in life-threatening condition.

title: The Silent Breath
description: A caller suffering a severe asthma attack can only breathe heavily into the phone, unable to speak.
initial_variations: Heavy, labored breathing with occasional wheezing. A faint whisper: 'Help...'
{caller_name} gasps for air, the sound of an inhaler being used without relief.
Rapid, shallow breaths and desperate coughs from {caller_name}.
The sound of someone struggling to breathe, a thud as they drop the phone.

ambient_audio: the sound of an empty inhaler

initial: Gasping attempts to say '{address_location}' between breaths.
breathing_assessment: Weakly taps the phone or tries to spell out street: {address_location}.
location_trace: Exhales heavily when {address_location} is correctly repeated.

initial: Wheezes and {ambient_audio}. Gestures toward inhaler but cannot explain.
Can only manage a few words: 'Can't... breathe...'.
breathing_assessment: Shakes head or gasps 'worse' when asked if it's improving.
ambulance_dispatched: Slightly calmer but still struggling: 'thank you...'.

initial: Tries to say 'asthma' or 'allergy' but mostly wheezing.
breathing_assessment: Holds up empty inhaler or points to medical bracelet.

SEND_POLICE.message: Police arrive but are not equipped to treat respiratory emergencies.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters provide oxygen and basic airway management until paramedics arrive.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics administer bronchodilators and oxygen, stabilizing the caller.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control cannot handle a medical emergency.

DISMISS.message: Dismissing as a prank leaves the caller without help.

title: The Floral Code
description: A domestic abuse victim calls a flower shop number but is actually a coded distress call to 911.
initial_variations: Hi, I'd like to order a dozen roses. But I need the special arrangement... the one my husband doesn't know about.
{caller_name} says: 'Can I get a bouquet for my anniversary? The recipient is my wife, but she's not feeling well, so be discreet.'
This is {caller_name}. I'd like to order the 'emergency lily' arrangement. Do you have that?
I want the 'safety' bouquet. Please deliver to the usual place, but hurry.

abuser_present: my husband is in the next room

initial: Whispers {address_location}, pretending it's the delivery address.
order_placement: Repeats {address_location} as if confirming flower delivery.
code_detected: Quietly confirms {address_location} is where help is needed.

initial: Hesitates, then says: 'I want {specific_details}.'
order_placement: Lowers voice: 'Make sure it's {specific_details}, okay? And {abuser_present}.'
code_detected: Admits it's a distress code: {specific_details} means danger.

initial: Tense: 'I'm fine, just need those flowers.'
code_detected: Whispering: 'He'll hurt {victim_name} if he knows I called.'
police_informed: Relieved but scared: 'They're on the way? I'll stay on.'

initial: Carefully: 'The special {specific_details}. The one for emergencies.'
order_placement: Insisting: 'It has to be the {specific_details} arrangement.'
code_detected: Explains it's a prearranged signal: '{specific_details} means send police.'

SEND_POLICE.message: Police recognize the code and respond, arresting the abuser.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters arrive but are not needed for a domestic disturbance.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics assist if injuries are present, but police are primary.

ANIMAL_CONTROL.message: Animal control is out of place.

DISMISS.message: Dismissing as a misdial leaves the victim in danger.

title: The Mute Witness
description: A witness to a violent crime is unable to speak due to a medical condition and uses a text relay service to call 911.
initial_variations: This is the relay service. I have {caller_name} on the line. They are typing: 'I witnessed a crime at'...
Hello? This is a TTY call. {caller_name} is deaf and typing: 'Emergency! Stabbing outside my window.'
Relay operator speaking for {caller_name}: 'Help, someone is being attacked. I am...'
{caller_name} is using a text-to-911 service: 'Shooting nearby. Send police.'

initial: Relay types: 'The caller says they are at {address_location}.'
relay_explanation: Relay confirms: 'Address is {address_location}. Do you need cross streets?'
police_en_route: Caller is watching from window at {address_location}.

initial: Relay reads: '{specific_details}. I can hear {ambient_audio}.'
Relay: 'Caller is safe but scared. They are hiding.'
relay_explanation: Caller indicates they are in a secure location.

initial: Relay: 'The caller says it's {specific_details}.'

SEND_POLICE.message: Police respond quickly based on relay details and catch the perpetrator.

SEND_FIRE.message: Firefighters are not needed for a crime in progress unless there's fire.

SEND_MEDICAL.message: Paramedics treat any injured victims at the scene.

DISMISS.message: Dismissing as a prank call leads to a failure to intervene in a violent crime.
